DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF HARRY FISCHER'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF THE SANTA BARBARA AUTO GROUP EMPLOYEES. PLAINTIFF HARRY FISCHER JR.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF THE SANTA BARBARA AUTO GROUP EMPLOYEES IS, BASED ON MOVING PARTY'S NOTICE THAT THE MOTION IS WITHDRAWN, OFF CALENDAR. MOVING PARTY IS ADVISED THAT STACKED DISCOVERY MOTIONS ARE DISFAVORED. GOING FORWARD, STACKED MOTIONS WILL BE ORDERED OFF CALENDAR IN FAVOR OF INDIVIDUAL MOTIONS FOCUSED EACH ON A SINGLE DISCOVERY VEHICLE (E.G., FROGS, SROGS, RFPS, RFAS, DEPOSITION NOTICE) ADDRESSED TO A SINGLE, SPECIFIC PARTY. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions Of The Santa Barbara Auto Group Employees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Gabrielle M. Diamse, Esq. In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF HARRY FISCHER's Motion To Compel Depositions Of The Santa Barbara Auto Group Employees.

Plaintiff Harry Fischer Jr.'s unopposed Motion To Compel Depositions Of The Santa Barbara Auto Group Employees is, based on moving party's notice that the motion is withdrawn, OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Party is advised that stacked discovery motions are disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
